title: Inconsistent behavior among browsers when clicking on buttons

quote.text: UNIX is simple.  It just takes a genius to understand its simplicity
quote.author: Dennis Ritchie
quote.authorTitle: Computer scientist and inventor of the C language

links.title: Inconsistent behavior among browsers when clicking on buttons
links.description: Browsers seem to be inconsistent in how they handle a click on a button element. Some browsers choose to focus on the button. Some browsers don't. Check out this interesting article if you are curious to know more about "yet another browser weirdness".

links.title: React Router v5.1
links.description: React Router 5.1 has recently been released and it comes packed with a lot of interesting changes. This new edition tries to incorporate learnings from **@reach/router** and it also brings new hooks like **useParams** and **useLocation**. Check out this article to learn about all the new shiny feature you can take advantage of.

links.title: Practical Ways to Write Better JavaScript
links.description: There are lots of simple things you can do to improve at JavaScript. Here are some methods we use to write better JS.

links.title: Moving beyond console.log()
links.description: 8 Console Methods You Should Use When Debugging JavaScript and Node.js.

links.title: JavaScript Iterators and Generators: Asynchronous Generators
links.description: All you should need to know about JavaScript Iteration protocol and Asynchronous Generators in 4 articles!

links.title: Nim language, v1.0 released
links.description: The Nim Team is very proud and happy to announce the much-anticipated version 1.0 of the language which makes it finally stable and good to be used for your next project.

links.title: A Senior Engineer's CheckList
links.description: A simple checklist, and while it is useful to any software engineer, it is especially useful to senior engineers as they need to be more aware of what to do to help the business and the colleagues grow every day.

book.title: Agile Web Development with Rails 5
book.author: Sam Ruby
book.description: Rails 5 and Ruby 2.2 bring many improvements, including new APIs and substantial performance enhancements, and the fifth edition of this award-winning classic is now updated! If you're new to Rails, you'll get step-by-step guidance. If you're an experienced developer, this book will give you the comprehensive, insider information you need for the latest version of Ruby on Rails.
